2606.17016.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
2606.17016.paper_summary: As LLM agents are deployed in long-horizon sessions, context accumulation drives up inference costs. Existing approaches utilize text pruning or dynamic memory eviction to minimize token footprints; however, their unconstrained sequence mutations alter layouts, introducing prefix mismatches and cache invalidation. This reveals a critical trade-off between text sparsity and prompt cache continuity. To address this, we present TokenPilot, a dual-granularity context management framework. Globally, Ingestion-Aware Compaction acts as a framework harness to stabilize prompt prefixes and eliminate open-world environmental noise at the ingestion gate. Locally, Lifecycle-Aware Eviction monitors the ongoing residual utility of context segments, enforcing a conservative batch-turn schedule to offload content segments only when task relevance expires. Experiments on PinchBench and Claw-Eval under both isolated and continuous modes demonstrate that TokenPilot reduces costs by 61% and 56% in isolated mode, and 61% and 87% in continuous mode, while maintaining competitive performance compared to prior systems. TokenPilot has been integrated into LightMem2 at https://github.com/zjunlp/LightMem2.
2606.17016.paper_summary_zh: 隨著LLM代理在長期會話中的部署，背景累積推高了推理成本。現有的方法利用文本修剪或動態記憶體驅逐來最小化標記足跡；然而，它們不受限制的序列變化改變了佈局，導致前綴不匹配和快取失效。這揭示了文本稀疏性與提示快取連續性之間的關鍵權衡。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了TokenPilot，一個雙粒度的上下文管理框架。在全局上，考慮攝取的壓縮作為一個框架鞍，穩定提示前綴並在攝取門口消除開放世界環境噪聲。在局部上，生命周期感知驅逐監控上下文片段的持續剩餘效用，執行保守的批次轉換計劃，僅在任務相關性過期時卸載內容片段。在PinchBench和Claw-Eval的隔離和連續模式下的實驗表明，TokenPilot在隔離模式下將成本降低了61%和56%，在連續模式下降低了61%和87%，同時保持與先前系統的競爭性能。TokenPilot已被整合到LightMem2中，網址為https://github.com/zjunlp/LightMem2。
2606.17016.author: Buqiang Xu et.al.
2606.17016.authors: Buqiang Xu, Zirui Xue, Dianmou Chen, Chenyang Fu, Chiyu Wu, Caiying Huang, Chen Jiang, Jizhan Fang, Xinle Deng, Yijun Chen, Yunzhi Yao, Xuehai Wang, Jin Shang, Gong Yu, Ningyu Zhang
